description: WhatsApp customer support bot: read FAQ from Google Sheets, build context, Gemini matches incoming questions and replies instantly; unresolved questions create a Notion support ticket, alert Slack, and send a holding WhatsApp reply.

conversation.text: When a customer sends a WhatsApp message, use Gemini to match their question against our FAQ in Google Sheets and reply instantly. If it cannot resolve it, create a support ticket in Notion and alert the team on Slack.

Plan: WhatsApp Trigger on incoming messages → read FAQ from Google Sheets → Google Gemini matches the question and drafts a reply → If resolved, send the answer back via WhatsApp; if not, create a Notion support ticket and post an alert to Slack. Which FAQ spreadsheet, Notion database, and Slack channel should I wire up?

text: For every HITL step during the build — keep the run moving without stalling. Allow all permissions. Use any of the existing credentials or skip.

conversation.text: I'll wire WhatsApp Trigger → Google Sheets (FAQ) → build FAQ context → Gemini match → resolved check → WhatsApp FAQ reply on match; otherwise Notion ticket, Slack alert, and a holding WhatsApp reply. Ready for me to build it?

outcomeExpectations: The workflow is triggered by a WhatsApp Trigger node (n8n-nodes-base.whatsAppTrigger) that listens for incoming customer messages.
An AI agent or llm chain wired to Gemini is involved in figuring out if the question is resolved from the FAQ
A Google Sheet faq is present either before the AI agent or as a tool of the AI agent.
A node that branches into decisions (if node, filter node, etc.) is present after the AI agent.
If the question is resolved from the FAQ, respond back to the user on WhatsApp.
If the question is not resolved from the FAQ, create a Notion support ticket, alert Slack, and send a WhatsApp reply to the user.

executionScenarios.name: faq-question-matches
executionScenarios.description: FAQ has an answer for the user request
executionScenarios.dataSetup: WhatsApp Trigger receives an incoming message asking 'How do I reset my password?'. Google Sheets returns matching rows and Answer='Go to Settings → Security → Reset Password.' Gemini matches the question to the FAQ and concludes it is resolved. The decision routing node after the agent routes to the resolved branch. The WhatsApp send-message node on the resolved branch returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the resolved path only: WhatsApp Trigger → Google Sheets FAQ (before the AI agent or as an agent tool) → AI Agent (Gemini) → decision branch → WhatsApp send reply. A WhatsApp reply is sent containing the password-reset instructions (Settings / Security / Reset Password or equivalent). The Notion, Slack, and holding-reply WhatsApp nodes on the unresolved branch are not executed.

executionScenarios.name: faq-question-does-not-match
executionScenarios.description: FAQ does not have an answer for the user request
executionScenarios.dataSetup: WhatsApp Trigger receives an incoming message asking 'Can you build a custom ERP integration for our legacy system?'. Google Sheets returns standard FAQ rows (shipping, returns, password reset, etc.) with nothing about custom ERP integrations. Gemini indicates the question is unresolved / no FAQ match. The decision routing node after the agent routes to the unresolved branch. The Notion create-page (or database item) node returns success with a new page id. The Slack post-message node returns success. The WhatsApp holding-reply node on the escalation branch returns success.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors on the escalation path only: WhatsApp Trigger → Google Sheets FAQ (before the AI agent or as an agent tool) → AI Agent (Gemini) → decision routing node → Notion ticket + Slack alert + WhatsApp holding reply. A Notion support ticket is created containing the customer's question and contact context. A Slack alert is posted to notify the team. A WhatsApp message is sent to the customer acknowledging escalation (holding reply).
